description: Align the Python SDK with backend theming and slideshow changes: update THEMES constant to 7 themes, fix slideshow guidance in prompts, deprecate background_color on slide() helper, add theme validation, update scaffold example.

userStories.title: Update THEMES tuple in capabilities.py to include all 7 themes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the SDK capabilities to advertise all 7 available themes so agents and validation logic know what's valid.
acceptanceCriteria: THEMES tuple updated from ('default', 'dark') to ('default', 'dark', 'executive', 'financial', 'consulting', 'technical', 'editorial')
No other constants in capabilities.py are changed
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Update prompts.py slideshow guidance and theme references
userStories.description: As an agent developer, I need the system prompt builder to give correct slideshow instructions (no background_color) and mention all 7 themes.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove all references to manually setting background_color on slides (e.g. 'Use a dark background_color (e.g. #0f172a) for the title slide')
Replace with guidance that themes auto-assign slide backgrounds: title slides (summary-header) get dark bg, content slides get light bg, closing slides (action-items/key-takeaway) get accent bg
Add mention of all 7 themes with brief personality descriptions in the format/theme guidance section
Slideshow instructions still recommend content_type='slideshow' and slide wrappers with summary-header for title slides
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Deprecate background_color param on slide() helper in sections.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the slide() helper to stop encouraging background_color usage since the backend now ignores it.
acceptanceCriteria: slide() function still accepts background_color parameter for backwards compatibility but defaults to None instead of '#ffffff'
When background_color is None, the returned dict does NOT include background_color key
When background_color is explicitly passed, it is still included in the dict (backwards compat) but a comment notes it is ignored by the backend
sections_reference() output updated: slide section description mentions theme-controlled backgrounds, does not list background_color as a field
sections_reference() includes a note about the 7 available themes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add theme name validation to validation.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I need local validation to catch invalid theme names before reports are sent to the server.
acceptanceCriteria: New validation check: if theme is provided and not in THEMES (from capabilities.py), add a warning
Warning message includes the list of valid theme names
Severity is 'warning' not 'error' (server falls back to default, so it's not fatal)
If theme is None or not provided, no warning is raised
validate_report() or the main validation function accepts an optional theme parameter
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Update scaffold.py example to showcase themes and slideshow
userStories.description: As an agent developer, I need the scaffolded example agent to demonstrate theme selection and include a comment about slideshow usage.
acceptanceCriteria: Scaffolded agent.py example uses theme='corporate' instead of theme='default' (more explicit)
Comment added near the publish call listing available themes: corporate (default), dark, executive, financial, consulting, technical, editorial
Comment added showing how to create a slideshow: content_type='slideshow' with slide() wrappers and summary_header() for title slide
Import list in scaffolded agent.py adds slide and summary_header to the imports from openreporting.sections
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
